Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Jesus Quevedo-Torrero

Review 2
Course: CSCE4350
Quality: 4.0
Difficulty: 3.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: Not sure yet
Comment: Took him for summer 2025 for Fundamentals of Database Systems. Class is easy if you follow the instructions and submit every assignment on time(He also gives a non-penalty extension for almost every assignment ). The exams were a little harder since they were open-note. Overall, it's an easy A or B if you watch all the lecture videos.
Tags: EXTRA CREDIT, Group projects, Lots of homework

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Jiang Beilei
School: University of North Texas
Department: Computer Science
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2870717
Overall Quality: 5
Number of Ratings: 6
Would Take Again: 100%
Level of Difficulty: 2.5

Review 1
Course: CSCE2610
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty: 2.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A+
Comment: Long, dense lectures. Occasional in-class assignments, but there was always proper warning beforehand. Recitations were optional. Homework could be long, but plenty of time was given and she has a 24 hour no-penalty late policy. Exams were very reasonable and she was clear about what would be on them (you get a cheat sheet too). Overall great/easy.
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Caring, Online Savvy

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Jiang Beilei
School: University of North Texas
Department: Computer Science
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2870717
Overall Quality: 5
Number of Ratings: 6
Would Take Again: 100%
Level of Difficulty: 2.5

Review 2
Course: CSCE5933
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty: 2.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A+
Comment: Took her Quantum Computing class (Topics in CSCE) in Fall 2025. Excellent class to get started with Quantum Computing. Dr. Jiang Beilei introduced all fundamental topics in Linear Algebra, Complex Numbers and Quantum Mechanics before actually diving into Core Quantum Computing, this helped all the beginners. Qiskit Labs were really useful.
Tags: Amazing lectures, Gives good feedback, Inspirational

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Kirill Morozov
School: University of North Texas
Department: Computer Science
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2402677
Overall Quality: 3.1
Number of Ratings: 5
Would Take Again: 67%
Level of Difficulty: 3.1

Review 1
Course: CSCE4050
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty: 3.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A+
Comment: Dr. Morozov is a fantastic professor. He explains concepts incredibly well and breaks them down so anyone can understand it. He encourages questions as much as possible and it's very evident that he cares that we understand the content not just pass his class. He has a unique exam style, but its 100% doable if you study.
Tags: Caring, Lecture heavy, Accessible outside class

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Kirill Morozov

Overall Quality: 3.1

Review 2
Course: CSCE4050
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty: 3.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A+
Comment: He is one of the best teachers at UNT, gives lots of homeworks and opportunities to obtain a good grade in his classes. He is extremely fair with the grading, and is always willing to talk to students that need some extra help (even outside office hours and with appointment). If you get a not so satisfying grade with him it's entirely a you problem
Tags: EXTRA CREDIT, Gives good feedback, Inspirational

Source: Rate My Professors

Review 1
Course: CSCE5210
Quality: 4.0
Difficulty: 4.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Would Take Again: yes
Grade: A
Comment: He is good at what he teaches. doesn't wrong you for what you say, explores your path and approach too. his mid exams are pen and paper based(cheat sheet allowed) but his sem are purely mcqs. he adjusts the grade according to the mean too. subject may be a bit tough but if you do some work you can score.
Tags: Group projects, Amazing lectures, Lecture heavy

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Ryan M. Garlick
School: University of North Texas
Department: Computer Science
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/272533
Overall Quality: 3.8
Number of Ratings: 58
Would Take Again: 81%
Level of Difficulty: 2.2

Review 1
Course: CSCE4350
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty: 1.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Not Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A
Comment: Material is all online, it's mainly quizzes and screenshot of assignments. The quizzes are all on quizlet, and the midterm is a combination of the previous quizzes. The assignments can be lengthy but they teach you real world examples of how to deploy/make use of the tools. You can finish all 16 weeks of work in less than 4 if you wanted to.
Tags: Clear grading criteria, Respected, Online Savvy

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Stephanie Ludi
School: University of North Texas
Department: Computer Science
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2227359
Overall Quality: 3.7
Number of Ratings: 6
Would Take Again: 67%
Level of Difficulty: 2.5

Review 1
Course: CSCE4444
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty: 2.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A+
Comment: I had Professor Ludi for CSCE4901 and now CSCE4902, she is a great professor. After a few weeks you get assigned a day with your group and you come in on that day. Most people don't come in unless its a meeting week (every 4 weeks or so). The rubrics are very clear, just follow them and its an easy 100. Make sure to pick good teammates!
Tags:Group projects, Clear grading criteria, Gives good feedback

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Stephanie Ludi
School: University of North Texas
Department: Computer Science
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2227359
Overall Quality: 3.7
Number of Ratings: 6
Would Take Again: 67%
Level of Difficulty: 2.5

Review 2
Course: CSCE4444
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty:3.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A+
Comment: Dr. Ludi is a great professor if you are okay with group work. The class is a breeze if you put the work in. Her lectures can get a little dry, but they are full of useful information. Prepare to work hard for her class if you want an A, but if you want to slack off you could probably get a C with minimal work.
Tags: Respected, ACCESSIBLE OUTSIDE CLASS, GRADED BY FEW THINGS

Source: Rate My Professors
Professor: Tanmay Bera
School: University of North Texas
Department: Computer Science
Source URL: https://www.ratemyprofessors.com/professor/2969350
Overall Quality: 4.9
Number of Ratings: 78
Would Take Again: 97%
Level of Difficulty: 3

Review 1
Course: CSCE-5210
Quality: 5.0
Difficulty: 5.0
For Credit: Yes
Attendance: Mandatory
Would Take Again: Yes
Grade: A
Comment: I'm genuinely grateful to have taken his class. Not only is he an excellent teacher, but he also cares deeply about his students' growth. His guidance, including writing me a recommendation letter, played a role in helping me secure a job. His lectures are practical and insightful. Just be honest—he does not tolerate academic dishonesty at all.
Tags:Participation matters, Amazing lectures, Caring

Comment:The professor demonstrated strong teaching abilities with clear explanations and engaging lectures. The syllabus was well-structured, covering key concepts thoroughly. However, the lecture handouts were overly long, making it challenging to focus on key points. Concise summaries would help.
